凤.origin: 凤 means phoenix, a beautiful magical bird in Chinese stories.

sentence.en: The phoenix is a beautiful bird.

凫.origin: 凫 is a wild duck; it has the bird 鸟 inside it.

sentence.en: There is a wild duck in the water.

凭.origin: 凭 means to lean on or to rely on, like resting against a table.

sentence.en: He succeeded by relying on hard work.

凳.origin: 凳 is a stool or bench you sit on.

sentence.en: I sit on the little stool.

凶.origin: 凶 means fierce or wicked; picture danger trapped in a pit.

击.origin: 击 means to strike or hit, the simplified form of 擊.

sentence.en: He strikes the drum with his hand.

函.origin: 函 means a letter; imagine a message placed in a box.

凿.origin: 凿 is a chisel, a tool that drills holes.

sentence.en: He drills a hole with a chisel.

刁.origin: 刁 means tricky or sly, like a cunning person.

word.en: to make things hard for someone

刃.origin: 刃 is the sharp edge of a knife 刀; the dot marks the blade.

刊.origin: 刊 means to publish or a periodical, using the knife radical.

刘.origin: 刘 is a common Chinese family name.

则.origin: 则 means a rule; imagine laws carved with a knife.

删.origin: 删 means to delete, like cutting passages out of a book 册.

判.origin: 判 means to judge or decide, with the knife radical.

sentence.en: The teacher judges right and wrong.

刨.origin: 刨 is a carpenter's plane that shaves wood smooth.

sentence.en: He planes the wood with a plane.

刮.origin: 刮 means to scrape or to shave with a blade.

刷.origin: 刷 means to brush, picture wiping with a cloth 巾.

sentence.en: I brush my teeth every day.

刹.origin: 刹 means to brake or stop; it also names a temple.

刺.origin: 刺 means to stab or a thorn, with the knife radical.

sentence.en: There are thorns on the rose.

剁.origin: 剁 means to chop by pounding, like mincing food.

剂.origin: 剂 means a dose, often of medicine.

sentence.en: He took a dose of medicine.

剃.origin: 剃 means to shave with a blade.

sentence.en: Dad goes to get a haircut.

削.origin: 削 means to pare or peel with a knife.

剐.origin: 剐 means to cut or scrape away.

剑.origin: 剑 is a sword, a long double-edged blade.

sentence.en: He holds a sword in his hand.

剔.origin: 剔 means to pick out, like removing bones from fish.

sentence.en: She picks out the fish bones.

剖.origin: 剖 means to cut open or dissect.

sentence.en: The doctor cuts open the watermelon.

剜.origin: 剜 means to scoop or gouge out with a blade.

sentence.en: He cuts out the rotten part.

剥.origin: 剥 means to peel or strip off a skin.

sentence.en: I peel a banana to eat.

剧.origin: 剧 means a play or drama.

sentence.en: We go to see a play.

剪.origin: 剪 means scissors or to cut, with the knife 刀 below.

副.origin: 副 means deputy or a set; it can also count pairs.

sentence.en: He wears a pair of glasses.

割.origin: 割 means to cut or divide with a knife.

剿.origin: 剿 means to wipe out or destroy.

sentence.en: The army goes to wipe out the bandits.

劁.origin: 劁 means to castrate or neuter an animal.

劈.origin: 劈 means to chop or split apart with a blade.

劐.origin: 劐 means to slit or cut open.

sentence.en: He made a cut in the cloth.

劣.origin: 劣 means poor or inferior; less 少 strength 力 makes it weak.

劫.origin: 劫 means to rob or a disaster.

sentence.en: The bad guy wants to rob.

勒.origin: 勒 means to strangle or pull tight with a strap.

勾.origin: 勾 means to hook or to draw a check mark.

sentence.en: Please put a check mark next to the right one.

勿.origin: 勿 means do not; it is a polite way to say must not.

匀.origin: 匀 means even or to divide equally.

包.origin: 包 means to wrap or a bag, like swaddling a baby.

sentence.en: I carry a schoolbag on my back.

化.origin: 化 means to change or transform.

匠.origin: 匠 is a craftsman, picture a tool 斤 in a box 匚.

匡.origin: 匡 means to correct or assist; it is also a surname.
